name: Hallmark
tagline: Taste, encoded as an agent skill
description: A design skill for Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex that pushes back on generic AI output: 22 themes across four genres, four verbs (build, audit, redesign, study), a 65-gate quality checklist, and per-project memory to stop repetitive design choices. Pure HTML/CSS/JS, no build step.
why: From Hassan El Mghari (Nutlope), a prolific open-source AI builder — an early, concrete attempt to make agent-generated interfaces not look agent-generated. 'Taste as code' is either a beautiful idea or a category error, and watching which is half the fun.
useCases: Better front-end output from coding agents
Auditing an existing UI for genericness
audience: Developers shipping front-ends through AI coding agents

setup.requirements: Install as a skill in Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex

license.notes: A skill/ruleset package rather than a conventional library.

limitations: Brand new, tiny, and opinionated — its aesthetics are its author's. Whether encoded taste survives contact with a thousand different projects is an open question.
take: The smallest project in this catalog and one of the most provocative: a bet that design judgment can be versioned.
